zh.lifeSummary: 當Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY於1388年出生在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯賀尼-波奧蒙特時，他的父親Guillebert I de  Lannoy58歲，母親Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix59歲。他和Catherine de MORLEMBAIX至少有1個兒子。他去世於1474年5月31日在他的家鄉享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY于1388年出生在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯帕-德-卡拉斯賀尼-波奧蒙特时，他的父亲Guillebert I de  Lannoy58岁，母亲Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix59岁。他和Catherine de MORLEMBAIX至少有1个儿子。他去世于1474年5月31日在他的家乡享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: LANNOY
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: altered form of Flemish Van Oye or Van Hoye (or perhaps an Americanized form of any of their French variants, such as Vanoye, Vannoye, and Vanhoye), a habitational name for someone from a place called with Middle Dutch oye ‘(water) meadow’, as for example Hoye, near Ghent, Belgium. This form of the surname has apparently died out in France. Compare Van Hoy and Vanoy .

History: According to the family tradition, the ancestors of Vannoy family were Huguenots from France who first settled in the Netherlands and later moved to North America. However, their surname is not listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors, nor in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vanoy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Ancil, Cecile.
American or French: variant of   Vannoy  , a surname of Flemish origin. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Van Hoy
meanings: American or French (also Vanhoy): variant of Vannoy , a surname of Flemish origin. This surname is very rare in France (Nord).

nameMeanings.name: Baudouin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Baldwin .

nameMeanings.name: dit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothy .

variants.name: Dorothy
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Profit’ or ‘sharp’.
2  ♀, ♂ ‘Upright’.

nameMeanings.name: bègue
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Christophe, Jean Louis.
French (Bègue): nickname for someone afflicted with a stammer, from the Old French adjective besgue ‘having a stammer’ (derived from Middle Dutch beggen ‘to chatter’).
French (Bégué and Begué): variant of Viguié, a status name and occupational name for a judicial officer, from Old French viguier (see Viger ).

variants.name: Beguin
meanings: French (also Béguin):
from a diminutive of the ancient Germanic personal name Bego, a short form of compound names based on the element beg, from Latin beccus ‘beak, bill’.

nickname from beguin, the name for a member of a medieval Christian male religious community (ultimately named after a priest called Lambert le Begue; see Begue ) that followed a monastic rule without making perpetual vows and was quickly considered heretic; by extension the term came to mean ‘sanctimonious person’.

variants.name: Viger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Laurent, Laurette, Lucienne, Marcell, Normand, Pierre, Simonne.
French: status name and occupational name from Old French viguier ‘seigniorial or royal officer responsible for administering justice and collecting state rights’ (from Latin vicarius ‘substitute’).

History: Antoine Viger from Saint-Malo in Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany (France), married Catherine Audet in Saint-Jean, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1751. Désiré Viger from Rouen in Seine-Maritime, France, married Catherine Moitié in Montreal, QC, in 1667.

en.lifeSummary: When Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY was born in 1388, in Hénin-Beaumont, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, his father, Guillebert I de  Lannoy, was 58 and his mother, Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix, was 59. He had at least 1 son with Catherine de MORLEMBAIX. He died on 31 May 1474, in his hometown, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY est né en 1388, à Hénin-Beaumont, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Guillebert I de  Lannoy, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix, avait 59 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Catherine de MORLEMBAIX. Il est décédé le 31 mai 1474, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY 1388 in Hénin-Beaumont, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillebert I de  Lannoy 58 und seine Mutter Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix 59. Er hatte mit Catherine de MORLEMBAIX mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 31. Mai 1474 im Alter von 86 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY è nato il nel 1388 nel Hénin-Beaumont, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, il suo padre, Guillebert I de  Lannoy, aveva 58 anni e il suo madre, Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix, aveva 59 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Catherine de MORLEMBAIX. Morì il 31 maggio 1474, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 86 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY(이)가 1388년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 he닌-보몽에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillebert I de  Lannoy(은)는 58세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix(은)는 59세였다. 그는 Catherine de MORLEMBAIX(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1474년 5월 31일 그의 고향에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY nasceu em 1388, em Hénin-Beaumont, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França, seu pai, Guillebert I de  Lannoy, tinha 58 anos e sua mãe, Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix, tinha 59 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Catherine de MORLEMBAIX. Ele faleceu em 31 de maio de 1474, em sua cidade natal, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY родился в 1388 г. в местности хeнин-бомонт, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillebert I de  Lannoy было 58 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix, 59. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Catherine de MORLEMBAIX. Он умер 31 мая 1474 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baudouin I dit le bègue de LANNOY nació en 1388, en Hénin-Beaumont, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, su papá, Guillebert I de  Lannoy, tenía 58 años y su mamá, Catherine de Corbon, dame de Molembaix, tenía 59 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Catherine de MORLEMBAIX. Murió el 31 de mayo de 1474, en su pueblo, a la edad de 86 años.
